patterns.reason: New ContextBuilder and ActionResolver modules must be readable; the OptionGenerator they replace was notoriously hard to follow.
ActionResolver's 7 action resolvers share validation patterns (position check, budget check) that should be extracted to helpers.
OptionGenerator (800+ lines, 10+ methods) was a god service — the entire v6.3 refactor exists to decompose it into focused modules.
OptionGenerator's failure came from trying to abstract enumeration + validation + pathfinding into one module. ActionResolver must stay focused on single-action resolution.
ActionResolver retry loop and TurnExecutor best-effort audit writes both involve error handling that must not silently swallow failures.
Codebase uses static methods with no DI. New modules should follow same pattern — avoid introducing unnecessary DI containers or abstract factories.
ActionResolver handles 7 action types. Each resolver should be purpose-built, not generalized into a plugin system.
LLM returns string action types that must be mapped to AIActionType enum. City names and load types from LLM response need validation against known values.

skipped.reason: Feature does not involve SSE streaming — bot turns are synchronous backend processes that emit socket events on completion.
Logging is not a primary concern in this refactor; existing DecisionLogger patterns are retained unchanged.
No frontend state management changes — StrategyInspector is display-only and receives data via socket events.
Boolean fields in interfaces (isLoadAvailable, success) are return values, not function parameters — the anti-pattern targets call-site obscurity.
